domain: Persian qanat (Kariz) underground gravity-fed water network — mother well taps aquifer; gently-sloping underground tunnel transports water passively over kilometers; vertical access shafts at intervals for maintenance and local extraction.
specific_mechanism: Gravity-driven continuous flow on a fixed gentle slope (0.3-0.5%) from a single mother-well source through underground tunnel, with periodic vertical access shafts providing intermediate maintenance points; no active pumping.
llm_application: QANAT-KV: KV-cache architecture with (1) a single 'mother-well' anchor — the deep-context state vector serving as long-term aquifer; (2) a fixed-slope priority-decay schedule along token position (analogous to gentle slope), passively decaying KV importance; (3) periodic 'access shafts' at every K tokens where low-importance KVs are evicted and the surviving stream is compressed into the trunk. No learned importance gating — passive flow architecture.
novelty_claim: LMCache (2510.09665) external KV cache for cross-request sharing. TRIM-KV (2512.03324) learns per-token retention. LKV (2605.06676) learns head-wise budgets. None propose a SINGLE-ANCHOR mother-well + FIXED-SLOPE passive decay + periodic access-shaft eviction-merge architecture without learned gating.
